response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with long tenure and broad language exposure, but he misses nearly every core requirement of this role: there is no evidence of native-level Japanese (languages listed are English, Romanian, and German), his C experience is limited to a brief seven-month IDE-tooling stint rather than sustained UNIX/embedded C development, and the resume shows no Linux kernel work, no L2/L3 networking or switch experience, and no security-technology background such as MACsec or 802.1X. The embedded exposure mentioned is peripheral (robotic arms, hardware tinkering) and does not translate to LAN-switch firmware ownership.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software development across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby using async I/O)
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No indication of native-level Japanese proficiency, a hard requirement for this position
Minimal C-for-UNIX experience and no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification background
No demonstrated knowledge of networking (L2/L3, switches, SNMP/NETCONF) or security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption/key exchange)

key_factors.factor: Lacks embedded networking and security domain experience
Long, credible general software engineering track record
